titlestmt.author: Compiled by G. Solomon, A. Gilbert

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-12-16 18:24:08 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes records of the Citizens' Commission of Inquiry on U.S. War Crimes and the Safe Return Amnesty Committee, both Vietnam War-era groups, and a successor of the two organizations, Citizen Soldier.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: Compiled by:
defitem.item: Michael Uhl Tod Ensign G. Solomon A. Gilbert M. Warren E. Engst J. Pactovis

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: The Citizens' Commission of Inquiry on U.S. War Crimes was an offshoot of the Bertrand Russell Peace Foundation. CCI staff organized the early chapters of Vietnam Veterans Against War (VVAW) in 1970-71. This work is the subject of two books: The Dellums Committee Hearings on War Crimes in Vietnam (Ensign, Rifkin, Uhl, editors, Vintage Book) and Standard Operating Procedure, by James Simon Kunen (Avon Books).

The Safe Return Amnesty Committee worked to win full amnesty for all military resisters to the war, the thousands of men who went AWOL or deserted and were given less-than-honorable discharges. This work is the subject of the book The Amnesty of John David Herndon by James Reston Jr. (McGraw Hill Books).

Citizen Soldier, a successor of the above two groups, has been in existence since 1976. CS has worked largely around issues of concern to active duty GI's, including unions, and has concentrated on veterans' health issues like Agent Orange and radiation exposure. Tod Ensign and Michael Uhl wrote the book GI Guinea Pigs (Playboy Books, 1980), which reports on the U.S. Defoliation program in Indochina and the Open Air A bomb test of the 1940s and '50s and the subsequent perception by veterans that these events have contributed to their decline in health.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Records include organizational records of Citizen Soldier and its predecessor organizations, the Safe Return Amnesty Committee and the Citizens Commission of Inquiry on U.S. War Crimes. Citizens Commission on U.S. War Crimes records, 1969-1974, include correspondence, news articles, publications, sample letters, reports, handouts, legal papers, and military hearings on war crimes in Vietnam and racism in the United States military. Safe Return Amnesty Committee records, 1969-1976, include correspondence, publications, sample letters, newsletters, handouts, press releases, photographs, transcripts of legal proceedings, film scripts, and agendas on amnesty and other national and international political issues of the time. Citizen Soldier records, 1968-1986, include correspondence, news articles, reports, newsletters, handouts, vitae, fundraising information, and letters to the editor on unionizing the military, and health issues, especially Agent Orange, Vietnamese prisons, and prisoners of war.

Also, files relating to legal cases involving military issues including health issues such as Agent Orange, exposure to nuclear testing, exposure to toxic chemicals, drug testing, suicides and deaths in suspicious circumstances, AIDS testing, andother health issues; civil rights issues such as blacks in the military, and gays in the military; military justice including a murder case in Panama, gun smuggling in Grenada, and covert operations; recruiting practices; conscientious objectors; and Vietnam veterans.
Part of Vietnam War Veterans Archives.
Materials relating to the death of Tod Ensign in 2014.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Incoming and chronological correspondence Boxes 1-7, 14-19, 23-25, 27
Series II. Organization/Date: Citizens' Commission of Inquiry on U.S. War Crimes, 1970-1971 Boxes 8, 29
Series III. Organization/Date: Safe Return Amnesty Committee, 1971-1975 Boxes 8-11
Series IV. Organization/Date: Citizen Soldier, 1966-2007 Boxes 11-29

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Citizen Soldier Records, #7033. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Series I. Incoming and chronological correspondence
June 27, 1988 U.S. Supreme Court: Delbert Boyle vs. United Technologies
E-mails regarding the death of Tod Ensign
Series II. Citizens' Commission of Inquiry on U.S. War Crimes
Commissions, GI vets, AGCS, Johnson letter, Spock letter, report
Letters to editor on atrocities (from civilians)
Index to proposal for Congressional hearings
Series III. Safe Return Amnesty Committee
Draft proposal, open letter to President Nixon
Political statements, correspondence, and press releases
Miscellaneous amnesty photos, handouts, reports, etc.
Transcript of proceedings: family testimony on amnesty
Transcript of proceedings: the effects of less-than-honorable discharges of Vietnam-era veterans
Miscillaneous Documents, inc. UP FROM EXILE
Veterans National Resource Project (NCC, Jack Smith)
L. Simon and Ed McNally case
Publicity, articles, etc. Safe Return Amnesty Committee, predecessor, Citizen Soldier (Vietnam Resisters) Amnesty Strugglee (see also Box 17)
Amnesty publicity, photocopied clippings, notes, etc.
First National Convention, Amnesty International, U.S.A.
Agendas, proposals, and drafts of papers
Sample of mail or comments hostile to amnesty
Diem, assassination, Hillsman memo, and related clippings
Institute of Industrial Relations, University of Calfornia at Los Angeles
Report to American Federation of Government Employees
Safe Return's contacts with GI support groups & unions in Europe
Army on Strike!, by M. Uhl and T. Ensign
National Toxicology Program Report on a,4-D, a,4,5-T, and TCDD
Book - "Guinea Pigs: American Veterans and the Abuse of Power"
Benton Musslewhite, Inc. - Agent Orange class action suit
Summit, Stuart law firm (Agent Orange initiative)
HERO (Hazards of electromagnetic radiation to ordnance)
AIDS and people of color: the discriminatory impact
Brisbois, John and Ft. Hood: HIV-III Aids-Testing
Civil rights - promotion case - Harris, Harold (1)
Bryan, Roberto Enrique (murder case in Panama)
Cassatt, Allen (gun smuggling case in Grenada)
Air Force Sexual Harassment Case, Pope AFB, NC
Columbia - U. S. Military Involvement - "narco terrorists"
Dependents (Schools, day care centers, health care, bowling alleys
Fundraising letters and flyers re: Safe Return and Amnesty for Vietnam Vets and Deserters
Fundraising letters and flyers re: Safe Return and Amnesty for Vietnam Vets and Deserters
Leaving the Military (benefits, VA programs, etc.
Petitions, Letters, etc. to Camilo Mejia
Pool of Tears - Greg S. Raisden
Tuition Aid: active duty Montgomery G. I. bill
Submissions to publisher: A Life Worth Living, Carl Feingold memoirs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: -"The Khaki Closet", "Real War Stories", "Addicted to War"
